method: in-app browser (Chromium) rendered evidence/ADP-S7-P02-T079/render_harness.html (built from the REAL worker base+HERO CSS, post-L1-cleanup build 9690390a9fc8) with worst-case data-dense content (104-char unbreakable URL as card text, long 文号/DOI, 8-col wide table, 1600px image), data-nav=topbar + data-theme=minimal (the nav.nav-top case). Metric: documentElement.scrollWidth <= innerWidth => no full-page horizontal scroll.

counterfactual_pre_fix_css.source: independent adversarial reviewer rendered the SAME worst-case content with the origin/main (pre-fix) CSS
counterfactual_pre_fix_css.note: proves the fix is load-bearing: without it the page scrolls 850px sideways; with it, 0px.

conclusion: Acceptance (1) no full-page horizontal scroll: PROVEN by real render at 360/390/430 (scrollWidth == innerWidth, 0 non-table offenders). (2) local table scroll: PROVEN (table display:block, clientWidth fits viewport, scrollWidth larger => scrolls inside its card). nav.nav-top does NOT overflow (candidate refuted empirically; navScrollWidth == navClientWidth == 320 at 360px).
